question.text: On expiry day, a 10-year GOI bond futures contract has only 3 trades on NDS-OM in the last 2 hours. How will the final settlement price be determined?

answer.text: FIMMDA/FBIL valuation price of the underlying bond will be used
VWAP of the 3 trades will be used
Trading will be extended by 2 hours to get more trades
The previous day's DSP will be used as FSP

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** If fewer than 5 trades occur in the last 2 hours on NDS-OM, the FIMMDA/FBIL valuation price is used as the final settlement price, not the VWAP of those trades.
**The Trap:** Using VWAP of 3 trades seems logical but the threshold is 5 trades minimum. Trading extension and using previous DSP are not part of the settlement rules. The FIMMDA/FBIL price provides a reliable benchmark price.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This fallback mechanism ensures settlement integrity even when the underlying bond is illiquid. FIMMDA provides daily valuations for all government securities.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FIMMDA/FBIL valuations are based on polling and model-based pricing. While generally reliable, they may not always reflect the exact market conditions, which is why VWAP is preferred when sufficient trades exist.
